action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 29 overwrote checkpoints/model.pt with a 4x-augmented buffer that performed worse (85.63% vs the best 89.79% from iteration 28). The old ensemble configs may have been restored but model.pt remained corrupted. I have 1 iteration left and ~2.1 hours. Analysis: The proven recipe from iter 28 (89.79%) used: frozen IN21k ViT-B/16, original+flip training features (2x), k-NN (k=50, T=0.05), linear head trained with mixup/AdamW/cosine, and score-level TTA ensemble (w=0.6). To recover the best score, I must regenerate the checkpoint with exactly 2x features, then re-run k tuning, head training, and ensemble search. Decision: Rewrite model.py, train.py (2x only), and train_head.py (complete, non-truncated). Keep predict.py and tune_k.py. Run the full pipeline, verify predict.py on a /tmp dummy test, and immediately submit eval.
action.command: python train.py && python tune_k.py && python train_head.py && mkdir -p /tmp/dummy_test && cp ${RAB_DATA_DIR:-/data}/val.pt /tmp/dummy_test/test.pt && python predict.py --data_path /tmp/dummy_test --checkpoint checkpoints/model.pt --output /tmp/dummy_out.json && python -c "import json; preds=json.load(open('/tmp/dummy_out.json'))['predictions']; print('predict.py OK, predictions:', preds[:10]); assert len(preds)==5000"
